eventMetadata.context_description: Tarik Skubal leads Polymarket's 2026 AL Cy Young trader consensus at 21.5% implied probability, bolstered by his back-to-back awards and elite early-season form—a 2.70 ERA, 45 strikeouts over seven starts, and 2.16 FIP despite not yet peaking—topping MLB's first Cy Young poll five days ago. Yankees rookie Cam Schlittler surges to 13.4% with a league-best 1.54 FIP and 2.49 ERA through seven outings, showcasing pinpoint control (one walk in 21.2 innings). Angels' José Soriano (11.8%) and Blue Jays' Dylan Cease (11.5%) tie for AL strikeout leads near 50 while posting sub-3.10 ERAs, differentiating via swing-and-miss stuff amid a wide-open rotation field lacking innings pacesetters yet.

markets.question: Will Racing Bulls get pole position at the 2026 F1 Canadian Grand Prix?
markets.description:
This is a polymarket on the constructor team that achieves pole position at the 2026 F1 Canadian Grand Prix, scheduled for May 23, 2026.

If the 2026 F1 Canadian Grand Prix is canceled or rescheduled to a date after May 30, 2026, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve in favor of the constructor team that is officially recognized by Formula 1 as having set the fastest time during the qualifying session for the 2026 F1 Canadian Grand Prix. The market will be settled based on the FIA's official qualifying results, regardless of any subsequent penalties, disqualifications, or changes to the starting grid.

For example, if a constructor team sets the fastest qualifying time but later receives a grid penalty or is moved down the starting order, the market will still resolve to “Yes” for that constructor team.

The resolution source will be the official Formula 1 website and a consensus of credible sports news reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mercedes' single-lap dominance, securing all poles through Japan with Kimi Antonelli and George Russell, has propelled them to the constructors' lead at 135 points after Miami, yet trader consensus prices every team near even at 49.5% for Canadian Grand Prix constructor pole position amid the 2026 regs' parity push. Circuit Gilles Villeneuve's razor-thin margins, punishing kerbs, and Wall of Champions chicane amplify unpredictability, favoring clean laps over raw pace as track evolution and clean air prove decisive. Ferrari trails closely at 90 points with strong Q3 threats from Leclerc and Hamilton, while midfield risers like Haas and Alpine eye upgrades; newcomers Audi Revolut and Cadillac lag but could surprise in low-downforce setup.

markets.question: US x Cuba military clash in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a military encounter between the military forces of the United States and Cuba between market creation and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A "military encounter" is defined as any incident involving the use of force such as missile strikes, artillery fire, exchange of gunfire, or other forms of direct military engagement between US and Cuban military forces. Non-violent actions, such as warning shots, artillery fire into uninhabited areas, or missile launches that land in territorial waters or pass through airspace, will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution. Intentional ship ramming that results in significant damage to (e.g., a hole in the hull) or the sinking of a military ship by another will count toward a "Yes" resolution, however minor damage (scrapes, dents) will not.

Note: the United States Coast Guard is considered part of the United States military, and the Cuban Border Guard is considered part of the Cuban military.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.description:

Non-violent actions, such as warning shots, artillery fire into uninhabited areas, or missile launches that land in territorial waters or pass through airspace, will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution. Intentional ship ramming that results in significant damage to (e.g., a hole in the hull) or the sinking of a military ship by another will count toward a "Yes" resolution, however minor damage (scrapes, dents) will not.

Note: the United States Coast Guard is considered part of the United States military, and the Cuban Border Guard is considered part of the Cuban military.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Trump's recent suggestion that the US could swiftly intervene in Cuba, coupled with mid-April reports of Pentagon contingency planning for potential operations, has escalated rhetorical tensions, prompting Cuban President Díaz-Canel to denounce "unprecedented" military threats and affirm readiness to defend sovereignty. Yet trader consensus favors "No" at 56%, reflecting ongoing high-level US-Cuba diplomatic talks in Havana as recently as late April, alongside Washington's focus on Iran de-escalation and Ukraine support amid allegations of Cuban aid to Russian recruitment. The February speedboat confrontation—where Cuban forces killed four on a US-flagged vessel attempting an armed incursion—triggered an FBI probe but no retaliatory actions, while new sanctions and oil curbs add pressure without crossing into direct military clash. Russia-Cuba military cooperation, ratified last year, fuels US concerns but lacks evidence of bases or deployments shifting odds decisively before year-end.

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the IL-09 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the IL-09 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Following the March 17, 2026, primaries, Evanston Mayor Daniel Biss secured the Democratic nomination in Illinois' 9th Congressional District after defeating a crowded field of 15 challengers, while John Elleson emerged as the Republican nominee from a low-turnout primary. Trader consensus at 92.5% for Democrats reflects the district's strong partisan lean—historically D+14 Cook PVI—with consistent Democratic dominance in recent cycles, Biss's local incumbency advantages as mayor, and limited GOP resources or name recognition for Elleson. No general election polling exists yet, but forecasters rate it Safe Democratic. Upsets would require a Biss scandal, extraordinary Republican national midterm wave, or depressed Democratic turnout ahead of the November 3 general election.

markets.question: Brazzaville: Matyas Cerny vs Bor Artnak
markets.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Matyas Cerny and Bor Artnak in the Brazzaville, originally scheduled for May 5, 2026 at 5:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Matyas Cerny' if Matyas Cerny advances against Bor Artnak.

This market will resolve to 'Bor Artnak' if Bor Artnak advances against Matyas Cerny.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the ATP Challenger Brazzaville on clay, trader consensus prices Matyas Cerny at 50% implied probability against higher-ranked eighth seed Bor Artnak (No. 438 vs. Cerny's No. 705), reflecting a competitive balance where Cerny's stronger 2026 clay record (8-4) offsets Artnak's career clay edge (97-57) and recent higher-level exposure. Both arrive with mixed form—Cerny dropping qualifiers in Ostrava and Split after Zadar qual wins, Artnak exiting early in Abidjan 2 (4-3 ret.) and Mexico City despite qual successes—highlighting no clear momentum edge in their first head-to-head. Late injury reports, tropical weather impacts on the outdoor surface, or qualifier fatigue for either could shift odds, underscoring the upset potential in this Round of 32 matchup.

markets.question: Jeonbuk Hyundai Motors FC vs. Gwangju FC: O/U 3.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming K-League game between Jeonbuk Hyundai Motors FC and Gwangju FC, scheduled for May 5 at 1:00 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if Jeonbuk Hyundai Motors FC and Gwangju FC combine to score 4 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 4, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on kleague.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Jeonbuk Hyundai Motors FC vs. Gwangju FC - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the K-League game, scheduled for May 5 at 1:00 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Jeonbuk Hyundai Motors hold a commanding historical edge over Gwangju FC, winning 22 of 33 head-to-head matches, as traders assess their upcoming K League 1 clash on May 5 at Jeonju World Cup Stadium. Their Round 3 encounter on March 14 ended in a 0-0 stalemate, but Jeonbuk has since built momentum with recent victories over FC Anyang and Daejeon Citizen, positioning them around second or third in the early standings with solid defensive form. Gwangju languishes mid-table after a 3-0 defeat to Gangwon FC last weekend, exposing away vulnerabilities. No major injury news from official reports; home advantage and Jeonbuk's superior recent form drive trader consensus on a competitive but tilted matchup.

events.title: Bitcoin price on May 7?
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the final "Close" price of the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

markets.question: Will Hyperliquid dip to $32 in May?
markets.description:

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the HYPEUSDT Low prices available at https://www.binance.com/en/futures/HYPEUSDT, with the chart settings on "1m" for one-minute candles selected on the top bar.

Please note that the outcome of this market depends solely on the price data from the Binance HYPEUSDT trading pair. Prices from other exchanges, different trading pairs, or spot markets will not be considered for the resolution of this market.

markets.question: Will Airbnb, Inc. (ABNB) hit (LOW) $116 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for Airbnb, Inc. (ABNB) has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Airbnb, Inc. (ABNB) "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.ABNB%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.ABNB%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

events.title: What will Airbnb, Inc. (ABNB) hit in May 2026?
events.description: What will Airbnb, Inc. (ABNB) hit in May 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: Airbnb (ABNB) shares trade around $142, up 12% over the past month and near the 52-week high of $147, fueled by optimism ahead of the Q1 2026 earnings release on May 7. Q4 2025 results showed revenue growth accelerating to 12% year-over-year at $2.78 billion, beating estimates despite an EPS miss at $0.56, with full-year revenue reaching $12.2 billion on robust gross bookings and nights booked expansion. Analyst consensus rates the stock overweight with an average 12-month price target of $150, reflecting expectations for continued travel demand recovery and AI-driven innovations. Q1 guidance of $2.59–$2.63 billion revenue (14–16% growth) sets the bar; outperformance or upbeat Q2 outlook could propel shares higher by May's end, while softening occupancy or macro headwinds pose risks.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Seattle be between 74-75°F on May 5?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Seattle-Tacoma International Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 5 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Seattle-Tacoma International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/wa/seatac/KSEA.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Latest National Weather Service forecasts and model ensembles, including GFS and ECMWF, indicate a Seattle high temperature of 73-75°F on May 5, reflecting trader consensus with 37% implied probability for 74-75°F amid an upper-level ridge fostering subsidence, clear skies, and warm advection aloft. The close contest with 72-73°F at 29% stems from uncertainty in Puget Sound marine layer persistence—prolonged stratus could cap peaks lower, while full afternoon mixing might push toward 76-77°F (18%). This setup marks above-normal warmth versus early May climatology (~64°F average highs), following May 4's forecasted 79°F peak. New 12z model runs today and NWS updates will sharpen resolution criteria tied to official Sea-Tac observations.

markets.question: Spread: Cultural y Deportiva Leonesa (-2.5)

events.title: Albacete Balompié vs. Cultural y Deportiva Leonesa - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the La Liga 2 game, scheduled for May 9 at 10:15 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Albacete Balompié hosts relegation-battling Cultural y Deportiva Leonesa in LaLiga 2 on May 9 at Estadio Carlos Belmonte, with trader consensus likely favoring the mid-table hosts due to home advantage and Leonesa's dismal form. Leonesa sit 22nd after recent defeats, including 2-1 at Mirandés on April 26, 1-2 home loss to Córdoba on April 18, and 1-0 at Granada on April 12, leaving them desperate for points in a must-win survival push amid their 8-8-20 record. Albacete, 13th in the table, lost 0-3 at Eibar on April 24 but won 4-1 versus Granada prior, showing attacking threat at home. Head-to-head history tilts toward Leonesa with two prior wins and three draws, though low-scoring (0.8 goals average); no major injuries reported, but long-term absences like Albacete's Antonio Puertas persist. Late-season stakes heighten upset potential for the visitors.

If the 2026 MLB season is cancelled, postponed after December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

events.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Tigers shortstop Kevin McGonigle leads AL Rookie of the Year trader consensus at 38.5% implied probability, edging White Sox first baseman Munetaka Murakami's 36%, as both deliver standout early-season performances through 30 games. McGonigle tops AL rookies in batting average (.333), on-base percentage (.420), slugging (.518), hits, doubles, and triples, bolstered by a 12-game hit streak and 25 consecutive games reaching base, giving him a WAR edge around 2.0. Murakami counters with explosive power, tying the MLB home run lead at 11—including a three-run shot on May 1—and a 1.020 OPS despite a .256 average, fueling recent odds flips amid voter appeal for counting stats like homers and RBI. The bunched odds reflect traders' split on well-rounded contact skills versus headline-grabbing slugging in a deep rookie class, with momentum hinging on sustained form, injuries, and schedule strength.

events.title: Hellas Verona FC vs. Como 1907

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors Como 1907 at 66.5% implied probability for the Serie A clash at Stadio Marc'Antonio Bentegodi, driven by their top-five table position, robust away form (third-best on the road), and Serie A-leading 16 clean sheets, contrasting Hellas Verona's 19th-place relegation scrap with just 18 points from 34 matches. Verona's winless streak reached six games after losses to Atalanta, Fiorentina, Torino, and Milan, plus a goalless draw versus Lecce, scoring only once in their last five outings amid key absences like Suat Serdar's season-ending cruciate tear, Daniel Mosquera's meniscus damage, and Daniel Oyegoke's ankle sprain; Gift Orban was also dropped for disciplinary reasons. Como holds recent head-to-head edge (two wins, one draw), though minor injuries like Jayden Addai's Achilles issue linger. Verona's lone home win this season tempers the draw at 19.5%, leaving them as 13% underdogs.

markets.question: Will Anže Logar be the next Prime Minister of Slovenia?
markets.description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Slovenia on March 22, 2026.

This market will resolve to the next individual who is officially elected by the National Assembly (Državni zbor) and sworn in as Prime Minister of Slovenia following the next parliamentary election.

To count for resolution, the individual must be formally sworn in. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister is sworn in by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Slovenia; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Next Prime Minister of Slovenia

This market will resolve to the next individual who is officially elected by the National Assembly (Državni zbor) and sworn in as Prime Minister of Slovenia following the next parliamentary election.

eventMetadata.context_description: Parliamentary elections on March 22 left Slovenia's National Assembly without a clear majority, with Robert Golob's Freedom Movement holding 29 seats to Janez Janša's Slovenian Democratic Party (SDS) at 28. Golob conceded failure to form a coalition on April 20, prompting President Nataša Pirc Musar to decline nominating a prime minister-designate on April 25 absent assured support. Trader consensus heavily favors Janša at 88.8% implied probability, driven by the National Assembly's recent passage of SDS-backed amendments trimming ministries from 19 to 14—securing 49 votes from a nascent center-right bloc including New Slovenia (Jernej Vrtovec at 3%)—signaling advancing coalition negotiations toward a confidence vote expected late May. Lower odds for alternatives reflect limited momentum amid deadlock.

markets.question: Will Paloma Valencia win the 2026 Colombian presidential election?

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate that wins this election. 

This market includes any potential second round. If the result of this election isn't known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket's Colombia presidential election market prices unidentified moderate Candidate M a narrow 49.5% favorite ahead of leftist Iván Cepeda Castro at 40.5%, with right-wing Abelardo de la Espriella (33.5%) and Paloma Valencia (27%) splitting conservative support in the May 31 first-round vote. Recent April polls (Invamer, GAD3, AtlasIntel) show Cepeda leading first-round intentions at 36-44% amid President Petro's recovering approval and Historic Pact's congressional gains from March 8 primaries, but escalating violence—including April 30 bombings killing dozens—has heightened security concerns, fragmenting the right and boosting moderates wary of polarization. Claudia López's April dropout and Cepeda endorsement bolsters the left, while upcoming debates could consolidate voters; further incidents or endorsements may tip the closely contested field toward a runoff.

Lindsey Graham commands 79% trader consensus to win South Carolina's Republican Senate primary on June 9, driven by his fundraising dominance exceeding $20 million, explicit endorsement from President Trump, and backing from Gov. Henry McMaster as state GOP chair. Challenger Mark Lynch trails at 14% after Paul Dans withdrew on April 10 and endorsed him, consolidating anti-incumbent support with Lynch's self-funded $5 million-plus campaign as a local businessman. Recent NYT polling average across six surveys shows Graham leading 43% to Lynch's 29%, but traders emphasize historical incumbency advantages in safe Republican primaries and Graham's institutional edge amid a fragmented field of over a dozen candidates. No major developments have emerged in the past week, with early voting approaching.

markets.question: Fed rate hike in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is increased at any point between January 1, 2026 and the Fed's December 2026 meeting, currently scheduled for December 8-9, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market may not resolve to "No" until the Fed has released its rate change decision following its December meeting.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official website of the Federal Reserve (https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm), however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects a 77% implied probability of no Federal Reserve rate hike in 2026, driven by the Fed's steady policy stance amid cooling inflation pressures and a resilient but balanced labor market. The federal funds rate has held at 3.5%-3.75% (effective 3.64%) through March and April FOMC meetings, with the March dot plot projecting a gradual path to the low-3% range by year-end 2027 rather than hikes. Recent March CPI data showed a 1.1% monthly rise but ongoing disinflation in core measures, supporting maximum employment without overheating. CME FedWatch aligns, pricing an 84% chance of no change through 2026-end; key catalysts include April CPI release on May 12 and the June FOMC.

In the upcoming game, scheduled for May 7, 2026
If CA Platense wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. events.title: CA Platense vs. CA Peñarol
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Copa Libertadores game, scheduled for Thursday, May 7, 2026 between CA Platense and CA Peñarol.

eventMetadata.context_description: CA Platense enters as trader consensus slight favorite at 46% implied probability for their Copa Libertadores Group E home clash against Peñarol, buoyed by a 2-1 upset victory in the first leg at Montevideo that vaulted them to second in the standings behind Corinthians. Peñarol's recent domestic losses and mounting injury crisis—key doubts over Ferreira, Darias, and Ángulo, plus long-term absences like Leonardo Fernández (knee) and Nahuel Herrera (shoulder)—have eroded their away threat to 24%, while conserving players for this pivotal fixture underscores its stakes for top-two qualification. Platense plans a near-identical lineup to their winning XI, with only minor tweaks like Gómez for Amarfil, amplifying home form in a closely contested matchup where draw pricing at 30.5% reflects defensive resilience on both sides.

markets.question: Will Chelsea Clinton be the democratic nominee for NY-12?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins the nomination for the Democratic Party to contest the NY-12 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The Democratic primary will take place on June 23, 2026.

If no nominee is announced by November 3, 2026, 11:59PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democrat sources, including https://democrats.org/.

Any replacement of the nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

events.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: In the open-seat NY-12 Democratic primary to succeed retiring Rep. Jerry Nadler, trader consensus slightly favors Assemblyman Micah Lasher at 40% over Alex Bores at 38%, with Jack Schlossberg at 22%, reflecting a fragmented field of over a dozen candidates and high undecided voters in early polls. Recent endorsements, including Gov. Kathy Hochul's April backing of former aide Lasher and Michael Bloomberg's prior support, have bolstered his position, while Bores benefits from assembly experience and a campaign poll showing strength. Candidate forums in mid-April sharpened policy contrasts on issues like Trump opposition, but no frontrunner has emerged amid split progressive-moderate lanes. AARP analysis highlights voters 50+ comprising up to 75% of turnout, keeping the race tight; separation could come from Working Families Party endorsement shifts, late fundraising reports, or final polls ahead of the June 23 contest.

markets.question: Will Nationalist Party win the most seats in the House of Representatives in the 2026 Maltese general election?
markets.description:
General elections are scheduled to be held in Malta on May 30, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the House of Representatives (parliament of Malta) as a result of the 2026 Maltese general election.

If the results of this election are not known definitively by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

Parties will primarily be ranked by the number of seats won. In the event of a tie, the party with the greater number of valid votes will rank higher. If a tie persists, the party whose name appears first in alphabetical order will rank higher. This market will resolve to the party that occupies the highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

This market’s resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the House of Representatives in the 2026 Maltese general election, not any coalition it may be a part of.

This market will resolve based on the results of the 2026 Maltese general election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve solely on the official results reported by the Government of Malta, such as those published by the Electoral Commission of Malta (https://electoral.gov.mt/).

markets.question: Will Le Mans FC achieve promotion from Ligue 2 to Ligue 1 for the 2026-27 season?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed team achieves promotion from the French Ligue 2 to Ligue 1 for the 2026–27 season at the conclusion of the current league season. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Official promotion is defined as a team clinching a place in Ligue 1 for the 2026–27 season through their final standing in the Ligue 2, via playoff victory, or by any other official league decision recognized by the Ligue 1 and Ligue 2.

If the 2025-26 Ligue 2 season is cancelled, postponed after June 12, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there are no teams confirmed for promotion within that timeframe, the corresponding market will “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official announcements from Ligue 1 and Ligue 2. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Ligue 2: Team promoted to Ligue 1
events.description:

Official promotion is defined as a team clinching a place in Ligue 1 for the 2026–27 season through their final standing in the Ligue 2, via playoff victory, or by any other official league decision recognized by the Ligue 1 and Ligue 2.

If the 2025-26 Ligue 2 season is cancelled, postponed after June 12, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there are no teams confirmed for promotion within that timeframe, the corresponding market will “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official announcements from Ligue 1 and Ligue 2. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Troyes has clinched automatic promotion to Ligue 1 as Ligue 2 champions after a dominant 4-0 victory over Laval on matchday 33, capping a stellar season with 67 points from 33 matches and the league's best goal difference of +28. Le Mans holds second place on 59 points, eight clear of Saint-Étienne and Red Star FC 93 (both 57), positioning them strongly for direct promotion ahead of the final matchday on May 8; a win over Bastia would seal it, though rivals could capitalize on any slip given tight head-to-head records and recent draws. Third through fifth enter promotion playoffs, with Rodez (55 points) surging via a 2-1 upset of Saint-Étienne, while goal difference and home form will prove decisive in the table's closing scramble.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket positions Austria's COSMÓ with "Tanzschein"—the host nation's national final winner—as the 27.5% implied frontrunner for last place in Eurovision 2026's grand final, reflecting lukewarm fan poll reactions and early rehearsal staging critiques despite automatic qualification advantages. The UK's experimental electronic entry "Eins, Zwei, Drei" by Look Mum No Computer trails closely at 22%, hampered by its quirky, polarizing appeal echoing the UK's historical televote struggles. Azerbaijan at 16.7% languishes after ranking dead last (0.42%) in the latest EN fan voting, with JIVA's power ballad "Just Go" drawing vocal concerns in first rehearsals. This tight cluster underscores competitive dynamics among Balkan and Caucasus acts, hinging on upcoming second rehearsals, semi-final televote momentum on May 12 and 14, and live staging refinements before the May 16 final.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mike Minogue's commanding 70% win at the Massachusetts Republican Party convention on April 26 secured the official GOP endorsement for the gubernatorial primary, propelling trader consensus to an 83% implied probability of his nomination and sidelining Mike Kennealy, who suspended his campaign after failing the 15% delegate threshold for ballot access. Brian Shortsleeve narrowly qualified at 15.5%, maintaining a competitive 13% market share by pledging debates and direct voter outreach ahead of the September primary. This delegate-driven consolidation reflects strong party establishment support for Minogue's business background and anti-establishment messaging, though Shortsleeve appeals to grassroots conservatives skeptical of convention insiders, with no post-convention polls yet shifting the dynamics.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus overwhelmingly favors Democratic Party of Korea's Park Chan-dae at 96% implied probability to win the June 3 Incheon mayoral election, reflecting recent polls showing him leading incumbent People Power Party Mayor Yoo Jeong-bok by margins exceeding 25 points, such as 54.9% to 29.5% in the latest surveys. Park's ties to President Lee Jae-myung, legislative experience as former floor leader, and pledges for an immigrant-friendly, economically vibrant Incheon have solidified ruling party momentum in this key battleground. Yoo's bid for a third term is hampered by an impending election law violation trial and voter fatigue after two terms. Late-breaking scandals, a favorable legal outcome for Yoo, or national polling shifts could challenge this dominance before early voting.

markets.question: Will Valtteri Bottas be the 2026 F1 Drivers' Champion?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

eventMetadata.context_description: Kimi Antonelli's commanding victory from pole at the 2026 Miami Grand Prix—his third consecutive win after China and Japan—has solidified his drivers' standings lead at 100 points, 20 ahead of Mercedes teammate George Russell on 80, driving trader consensus to price the young Italian at 39.6% implied probability for the title amid Mercedes' constructors' dominance with 180 points. Russell's consistent podiums but P4 in Miami highlight intra-team pressure, positioning him second at 26.5%, while McLaren's Lando Norris (11.3%) benefits from recent podiums despite trailing in standings at 51 points. Red Bull's struggles cap Max Verstappen at 5.4%, reflecting their lowly 30 team points and race pace deficits early in the season. With 18 races remaining, Mercedes' edge in qualifying and tire strategy fuels the frontrunners' favoritism.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors Partido Liberal (PL) at 85% to win the most seats in Brazil's October 4, 2026, Senate election, where 54 of 81 seats (two per state across 27 states) will be contested via plurality vote. Recent state-level polls, such as those showing dual PL leads in Santa Catarina (Carol de Toni and Carlos Bolsonaro topping intentions), project PL securing 20-30 seats, bolstered by the party's overtake of PSD as the Senate's largest bloc in January 2026 through defections. Momentum from Flávio Bolsonaro's tight presidential polls against Lula—statistically tied in April AtlasIntel/Bloomberg runoff scenarios—drives PL coattails, while UNIÃO Brasil (8.9%) and MDB (8.8%) show strength in fewer states like Goiás and Paraná. Further candidate filings and surveys could shift projections.

markets.question: Will annual inflation increase by ≤3.1% in April?
markets.description:
This is a market about inflation over the 12-month period ending April 2026, before seasonal adjustment, as reported by the Bureau of Labor Statistics.

This market will resolve to the percentage change in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) over the 12-month period ending in April 2026 according to the monthly Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) report.

The resolution source for this market will be the BLS Consumer Price Index report released for April 2026 (https://www.bls.gov/bls/news-release/cpi.htm), currently scheduled to be released on May 12, 2026, at 8:30 AM ET. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data.

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly BLS CPI news release, which reports inflation over 12-month periods to only one decimal point (e.g., 2.9%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

If the BLS does not release the relevant figures on the scheduled date, this market may remain open up until the scheduled release time of the next CPI report (https://www.bls.gov/schedule). If the information is not released by that time, this market will resolve according to the figures of the most recent previous month with available data.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders have priced April 2026 US CPI annual inflation in a tight contest, with 3.7% at 37.9% implied probability edging 3.8% at 30.9%, reflecting deep uncertainty following March's hotter-than-expected 3.3% year-over-year print—up sharply from February's 2.4% on a 10.9% energy index surge driven by Middle East conflict and gasoline prices jumping 21.2%. Recent oil pullbacks have tempered upside fears, but persistent shelter costs and base effects keep higher readings viable, differentiating outcomes. Consensus hinges on energy passthrough and core services momentum ahead of the Bureau of Labor Statistics release on May 12, underscoring prediction markets' real-capital sensitivity to these swings.

markets.question: Cavaliers vs. Pistons: O/U 214.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for May 5 at 7:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if the Cavaliers and Pistons combine to score 215 or more points in this game.

If the combined total is less than 215, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

events.title: Cavaliers vs. Pistons
events.description:

If the Cavaliers win, the market will resolve to "Cavaliers".
If the Pistons win, the market will resolve to "Pistons".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

eventMetadata.context_description: The top-seeded Detroit Pistons hold home-court advantage over the No. 4 Cleveland Cavaliers in the Eastern Conference semifinals, both advancing via Game 7 triumphs on May 3—Pistons dominating Orlando Magic 116-94, Cavaliers rallying past Toronto Raptors 114-102. Cavaliers enter with a clean injury report, stars Donovan Mitchell, James Harden, Evan Mobley, and Jarrett Allen fully available after managing ailments earlier; Pistons rule out Kevin Huerter (left adductor strain) but boast intact core of Cade Cunningham, Tobias Harris, Jalen Duren, and Ausar Thompson. Detroit's league-best 60-22 regular season and physical identity face Cleveland's depth and playoff momentum, with recent head-to-heads tight amid potential fatigue from extended first-round battles; Game 1 tips Tuesday at Little Caesars Arena.

eventMetadata.context_description: World No. 1 Jannik Sinner's implied 69.5% probability leads trader consensus for the 2026 French Open title, fueled by his dominant clay-court swing, including straight-sets Madrid Open victory over Alexander Zverev in the final just days ago and earlier Monte-Carlo Masters 1000 triumph after ousting Carlos Alcaraz. Alcaraz's withdrawal on April 24 due to a persistent right wrist injury—ruling out the defending champion and No. 2 seed—has cleared a major path, while Sinner extends his record 28-match Masters 1000 win streak and eyes a career Grand Slam at Roland Garros. Zverev (5.9%) gains from his Madrid runner-up finish and strong surface history, with Djokovic (6.3%) relying on experience amid limited recent form; rising Frenchman Arthur Fils (4.2%) and Spaniard Rafael Jodar (3.2%) represent dark-horse value on home clay.

events.title: Club Atlético de Madrid vs. RC Celta de Vigo - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the La Liga game, scheduled for May 9 at 12:30 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Atlético Madrid, entrenched in fourth place in the La Liga table after 33 matches, host sixth-placed RC Celta de Vigo on May 9 at Riyadh Air Metropolitano in a pivotal late-season clash for Champions League qualification. Los Colchoneros' recent form includes gritty home wins over Athletic Bilbao (3-2) and Getafe (1-0), bolstered by strong head-to-head dominance (19 wins in 30 meetings), though injuries to José Giménez (muscle), Dávid Hancko (ankle), and Pablo Barrios (thigh) temper squad depth. Celta, winless in their last five with a 1-0 loss to Barcelona, face away form woes despite a 1-1 draw at home earlier this season; key absences like Manu Fernández (muscle) add pressure amid their Europa League push. Home advantage and table stakes shape trader consensus.

markets.question: Will Analilia Mejia win the NJ-11 Special Election by 40% or more?
markets.description:
The special election for the seat for New Jersey's 11th congressional district in the U.S. House of Representatives is currently scheduled to take place on April 16, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the margin of victory between the top two candidates in the specified election.

For the purpose of this market, the “margin of victory” is defined as the absolute difference between the percentages of valid votes received by the first-place and second-place candidates. Percentages of the valid votes received by each candidate will be determined by dividing the total number of valid votes each of the top two candidates receives by the sum of all valid votes cast in the election.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher placed bracket.

If two or more candidates tie for the most valid votes in this election, and the tie is between two candidates listed, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the candidate whose listed last name comes first in alphabetical order. If the tie is between a listed candidate and an unlisted candidate, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the listed candidate. If the tie is between two or more unlisted candidates, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve based on the official vote count once the count has been made official.

If the results of the specified election are not known definitively by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official sources, including https://www.nj.gov/state/.

If a recount is initiated before the vote total has been made official, the market will remain open until the recount is completed and the vote is made official.

eventMetadata.context_description: With over 95% of precincts reporting from the April 16 special election in New Jersey's 11th Congressional District, trader consensus heavily favors a Mejia victory margin of 20-25%, reflecting stable tallies showing Democrat Analilia Mejia at 60.2% (81,825 votes) to Republican Joe Hathaway's 39.4% (53,520 votes), a 20.8-point lead. This commanding position stems from the district's Democratic lean—previously held by now-Gov. Mikie Sherrill—bolstered by Mejia's fundraising edge, progressive endorsements, and superior early voting turnout that outperformed 2024 benchmarks amid low special election participation. Final certification awaits lingering mail-in and provisional ballots, but a shift below 20% would require improbable Republican surges in Morris County strongholds like Pequannock, while exceeding 25% is unlikely absent minor other-candidate adjustments.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Lagos be 28°C on May 5?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Murtala Muhammad International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 5 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Murtala Muhammad International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/ng/lagos/DNMM.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket favors 34°C (31%) and 33°C (27%) as the highest temperature in Lagos on May 5, reflecting NiMet's February 2026 seasonal climate prediction of above-normal temperatures across Nigeria amid an early rainy season onset. Short-range forecast ensembles from global models project daytime highs around 33–34°C, driven by persistent upper-level high pressure, urban heat island intensification in Lagos, and moderating influences like Gulf of Guinea sea breezes and rising humidity. The close split stems from uncertainty in cloud cover and isolated thunderstorms, which could cap peaks at 33°C under partial overcast or permit 34°C with more sunshine; historical early-May highs average 31–32°C at official stations. Updated NiMet daily bulletins and 00Z/12Z model runs later today may shift odds further.

markets.question: Metamask FDV above $500M one day after launch?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Metamask's token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Metamask doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

markets.question: Bank of England decreases interest rates by 50+ bps after July 2026 meeting?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the change in basis points in the Bank Rate resulting from the July 2026 meeting of the Bank of England’s Monetary Policy Committee, relative to the level it was prior to this meeting.

The resolution source will be official information from the Bank of England, including the statement or release from its July 2026 Monetary Policy Committee meeting, scheduled for July 30, 2026, as listed on the official Bank of England calendar (https://www.bankofengland.co.uk/monetary-policy/upcoming-mpc-dates). This market may resolve as soon as the statement or release of the Bank of England's July 2026 Monetary Policy Committee meeting with relevant data is issued.

If the specified rate is defined by an upper and lower bound, the relevant change will be the change to the upper bound.

If the specified rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded according to the following guidelines. Increases or decreases of less than 25 bps will be rounded to 25 bps (e.g. an increase or decrease of 10 bps would be considered to be an increase or decrease of 25 bps). Increases or decreases of greater than 25 bps will be rounded to the nearest 25 bps and will be rounded away from 0 in cases of equidistance (e.g., an increase or decrease of 37.5 bps would be considered to be an increase or decrease of 50 bps). Displayed options of “Increase” or “Decrease” will include policy rate increases or decreases of any size.

If the specified meeting is postponed to a date and time before the start of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve based on the outcome of that postponed meeting. If the specified meeting is cancelled, or postponed such that no decision is announced by the start of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the “No Change” bracket. Emergency changes to the specified rate not resulting from the specified meeting will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders price evenly matched implied probabilities of 49.5% across all Bank of England July Monetary Policy Committee outcomes—from 25 basis points or 50+ bps hikes/cuts to no change—reflecting deep uncertainty over the rate path amid conflicting signals. March 2026 CPI accelerated to 3.3% year-over-year from 3.0%, fueled by energy and motor fuel spikes from Middle East conflict, leading to a unanimous MPC hold at 3.75% Bank Rate. Resilient labor conditions, with unemployment falling to 4.9% in February, counterbalance disinflation forces, while persistent services inflation sustains hike risks. Pivotal catalysts include today's April 30 decision, upcoming May/June CPI data, and energy price developments ahead of the July 30 meeting.

markets.question: US Cremonese vs. SS Lazio: O/U 4.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming Serie A game between US Cremonese and SS Lazio, scheduled for May 4 at 12:30 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if US Cremonese and SS Lazio combine to score 5 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 5, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on legaseriea.it. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: US Cremonese vs. SS Lazio - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Serie A game, scheduled for May 4 at 12:30 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Relegation-battling US Cremonese host mid-table SS Lazio at Stadio Giovanni Zini in a pivotal Serie A matchup, with the hosts' survival hopes hinging on ending a three-match winless streak after a 0-0 draw versus Torino on April 19. Cremonese's poor home form (2 wins, 6 draws, 7 losses) is compounded by key absences, including striker Jamie Vardy's ongoing thigh injury and Faris Moumbagna's adductor problem, thinning their attack. Lazio arrive with momentum from a 2-0 upset win at Napoli on April 18 but face defensive woes from Adam Marusic's calf strain and Mario Gila's knee issue. Lazio dominate head-to-head (3 straight wins), yet Cremonese's desperation and home edge signal a tightly contested affair amid both teams' injury-hit squads.

markets.question: Will the Bank of Mexico announce an increase at the June meeting?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the change in the target for the overnight interbank interest rate as a result of the monetary policy decision of the Bank of Mexico's June 2026 meeting versus the level it was prior to this meeting.

The resolution source for this market is information released by the Bank of Mexico after its policy meeting scheduled for June 25, 2026, as listed on the official Bank of Mexico calendar: https://www.banxico.org.mx/viewers2/JSP/calendarioDifusion_es.jsp

This market may resolve as soon as the Bank of Mexico's statement for their June meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

This market may resolve as soon as the Bank of Mexico's statement for their June meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket assigns an 84% implied probability to no change in Banco de México's (Banxico) target overnight interbank rate at its June 2026 meeting, following the surprise 25 basis point cut to 6.75% on March 26 amid a divided Governing Board vote and despite persistent inflation above the 3% target. Early April 2026 headline CPI eased to 4.53% year-on-year from prior highs, signaling modest disinflation that supports a potential pause after the expected May 7 rate reduction priced at over 90% in parallel markets. Governor remarks on March 30 highlighted the easing cycle nearing completion, with core price pressures and rising global yields reinforcing trader caution for further adjustments. The May decision remains the pivotal catalyst ahead.

markets.question: O/U 1.5 Rounds
markets.description:
Each market will resolve to “Over” if the fight between Yaroslav Amosov and Joel Álvarez at UFC 328: Chimaev vs. Strickland, scheduled for May 9, 2026, lasts beyond the listed round threshold.

Each market will resolve to “Under” if the fight between Yaroslav Amosov and Joel Álvarez at UFC 328: Chimaev vs. Strickland, scheduled for May 9, 2026, does not last beyond the listed round threshold.

For example:

“Over 1.5 Rounds” will resolve “Yes” if the fight continues past the 2:30 mark of Round 2.

“Under 1.5 Rounds” will resolve “Yes” if the fight ends before the 2:30 mark of Round 2.

If the fight ends exactly at the threshold (e.g., 2:30 of Round 2 for a 1.5 line), it will resolve “50-50.”

If the bout is not scored, postponed beyond May 23, 2026, or canceled, all options will resolve “50-50.”

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the UFC.

events.title: UFC 328: Yaroslav Amosov vs. Joel Álvarez (Welterweight, Prelims)
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yaroslav Amosov" if Yaroslav Amosov is officially declared the winner of the fight against Joel Álvarez at UFC 328: Chimaev vs. Strickland, scheduled for May 9, 2026.

It will resolve to "Joel Álvarez" if Joel Álvarez is officially declared the winner.

If the fight is declared a draw or technical draw, ruled a No Contest, not scored, canceled, or postponed beyond May 23, 2026, this market will resolve "50-50."

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the UFC.

eventMetadata.context_description: Yaroslav Amosov enters his second UFC bout as the welterweight prelims focal point against Joel Álvarez at UFC 328, where trader consensus has shifted sharply toward the former Bellator champion's elite wrestling pedigree following his dominant first-round anaconda choke submission of Neil Magny in December 2025. Álvarez, riding a four-fight win streak capped by a unanimous decision over Vicente Luque last October, brings 6'3" reach and lethal bottom-game submissions but faces stylistic peril against Amosov's relentless takedown chains and ground control at 170 pounds. No injury concerns reported ahead of Friday's weigh-ins, heightening focus on Álvarez's takedown defense versus Amosov's pressure in this closely contested matchup.

markets.question: Will Fernando Tatis Jr. win the 2026 National League Hank Aaron Award?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the player who wins the 2026 National League Hank Aaron Award.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by MLB rules. If multiple winners are announced then this market will resolve to the player whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2026 MLB season is cancelled, postponed after December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Major League Baseball; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors Mookie Betts at 26% implied probability for the NL Hank Aaron Award due to his elite on-base skills and prime spot atop the Dodgers' loaded lineup, despite a slow .179 batting average start with 2 HR and 7 RBI. Shohei Ohtani (20%) and Bryce Harper (19%) trail closely on strong power surges—Ohtani's .824 OPS with 6 HR and Harper's 19 RBI—while Juan Soto's .314 average and .915 OPS from the Mets lineup keep him viable at 14%. Ronald Acuña Jr.'s modest .740 OPS post-injury tempers his 10% share, as Kyle Schwarber's NL-leading 11 HR and .915 OPS fuel his rise but raise sustainability doubts in small-sample early season stats after ~30 games, maintaining a tightly contested field emphasizing full-season projection over hot starts.

events.description: More markets for the UEFA Champions League game, scheduled for May 5 at 3:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Arsenal hold a strong position heading into their UEFA Champions League semi-final second leg against Atletico Madrid at the Emirates on May 5, following a dominant 4-0 league-phase win over the Spanish side in October. Atletico's momentum has stalled after a heartbreaking Copa del Rey final penalty-shootout loss to Real Sociedad on April 18, where key forwards Ademola Lookman and Alexander Sorloth sustained injuries, casting doubt on their fitness for the first leg at the Metropolitano on April 29 and exposing defensive vulnerabilities. Leading the Premier League table, Arsenal contend with Martin Odegaard's ongoing knee issue and Bukayo Saka's ankle doubt ahead of their April 25 Newcastle clash, while Atletico sit fourth in La Liga amid a form dip—traders weigh home-soil resilience against Arsenal's attacking firepower and head-to-head edge.

markets.question: U.S. enacts AI safety bill before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a bill that includes at least one of the following provisions is signed into federal law in the United States  by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. 

- Prohibition on Creation or Release: Forbids the creation or release of specific AI systems or models.

- Training Restrictions: Sets limits on how AI systems can be trained, such as restricting access to previously available training data or imposing a maximum limit on the number of parameters used for training.

- Usage Restrictions:  Prevents AI systems from being used in certain applications, such as interacting with customers, interfacing with other applications, or performing actions on the web.

- Human-in-the-Loop Requirements: Requires AI systems to include mechanisms ensuring human oversight or involvement in their operation.

Otherwise this market will resolve to "No". 

The resolution source will be official U.S. federal government (e.g., Congress.gov) however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: U.S. enacts AI safety bill before 2027?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a bill that includes at least one of the following provisions is signed into federal law in the United States  by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. 

- Prohibition on Creation or Release: Forbids the creation or release of specific AI systems or models.

- Training Restrictions: Sets limits on how AI systems can be trained, such as restricting access to previously available training data or imposing a maximum limit on the number of parameters used for training.

- Usage Restrictions:  Prevents AI systems from being used in certain applications, such as interacting with customers, interfacing with other applications, or performing actions on the web.

- Human-in-the-Loop Requirements: Requires AI systems to include mechanisms ensuring human oversight or involvement in their operation.

Otherwise this market will resolve to "No". 

The resolution source will be official U.S. federal government (e.g., Congress.gov) however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects congressional gridlock hindering a comprehensive AI safety bill's passage before 2027, with No leading at 72.5% amid stalled federal efforts. The Trump administration's March 20 National AI Legislative Framework urged light-touch regulations prioritizing innovation, child protections, and state law preemption, but it has not catalyzed bipartisan momentum for stringent safety measures. Recent introductions like H.R. 8037 (Protect American AI Act, March 24) and a narrow Senate bill on AI chatbots for kids (April 28) remain in early committee stages, facing partisan skepticism and competing priorities ahead of November midterms. With the 119th Congress winding down, states are advancing their own AI rules while federal enactment appears unlikely without a post-election lame-duck push.

eventMetadata.context_description: Bert Mizusawa leads trader consensus at 47% implied probability for the Virginia Republican U.S. Senate primary on August 4, driven by his high-profile background as a retired U.S. Army major general, West Point graduate, and former senior government executive, bolstered by his early ballot qualification certified by the Republican Party of Virginia on April 7. David Williams (22%) and Kim Farington (16%) trail as fellow qualifiers, benefiting from confirmed ballot access amid pending litigation for others like Chuck Smith, while statewide figures such as Jason Miyares and Winsome Earle-Sears linger at low odds without formal entry. Absent public polls, markets reflect skin-in-the-game assessments of name recognition and organizational strength in this open field challenging incumbent Democrat Mark Warner.

eventMetadata.context_description: New York Liberty lead trader consensus at 27% implied probability for the 2026 WNBA championship following contract extensions for core stars Breanna Stewart and Sabrina Ionescu, ensuring roster continuity after their 2024 title and strong 2025 Finals push, with recent re-signings like Jonquel Jones bolstering frontcourt depth amid training camp. Indiana Fever sit second at 16% on rising momentum from Caitlin Clark and Aliyah Boston's dynamic duo, plus Commissioner's Cup win and solid free agency additions around their young core. Defending champions Las Vegas Aces, fresh off sweeping Phoenix Mercury for their third crown in four years with A'ja Wilson re-signed supermax, trail slightly at 15.5% as competition intensifies league-wide post-expansion. Atlanta Dream's 11% reflects retaining starters Rhyne Howard, Allisha Gray and Jordin Canada, differentiating via backcourt retention in a wide-open field with new entrants like Toronto Tempo and Portland Fire. Preseason tilts, including Fever's win over Liberty, underscore the parity.

markets.question: Will SpaceX's market cap be between $1.8T and $2.0T at market close on IPO day?
markets.description:
This market will resolve based on SpaceX's market capitalization at the closing price on its first day of trading.

If no SpaceX IPO occurs by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No IPO before 2028".

Market capitalization expresses the monetary value of a company’s outstanding shares, stated in its pricing currency. It is calculated as the number of shares outstanding multiplied by the closing share price on the first trading day.

If the relevant value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Resolution will be based on the primary exchange’s official listing page. In the event that the relevant figure is not displayed, another reliable source will be used.

In the event of an interruption in the course of the normal trading session on SpaceX’s first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that as the first day of trading for purposes of this market.

events.title: SpaceX IPO Closing Market Cap (Lower Strikes)
events.description:
This market will resolve based on SpaceX's market capitalization at the closing price on its first day of trading.

If no SpaceX IPO occurs by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No IPO before 2028".

Market capitalization expresses the monetary value of a company’s outstanding shares, stated in its pricing currency. It is calculated as the number of shares outstanding multiplied by the closing share price on the first trading day.

If the relevant value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Resolution will be based on the primary exchange’s official listing page. In the event that the relevant figure is not displayed, another reliable source will be used.

In the event of an interruption in the course of the normal trading session on SpaceX’s first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that as the first day of trading for purposes of this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: SpaceX's confidential IPO filing in early April 2026, targeting a June roadshow and prospectus release by late May, has driven trader consensus toward a 2.0 trillion+ closing market cap at 55% implied probability, reflecting Starlink's explosive growth to over 4.6 million subscribers and $12 billion annual revenue alongside Starship reusable rocket milestones like upcoming V3 and V4 flights. Recent tender offers valued the company at $800 billion in late 2025, but filings highlight ambitious multi-planetary and AI integration plans that could justify a record $1.75 trillion-plus debut, with Musk retaining voting control via super-voting shares. Lower brackets like 1.8T–2.0T at 13% account for execution risks in satellite constellation expansion and regulatory hurdles, while no-IPO-before-2028 odds remain slim at 3.6% amid momentum.

markets.question: Will Jorge Rodríguez be the leader of Venezuela end of 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the individual who officially holds the position of the head of state of Venezuela on Dec 31, 2026 at 12 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, "officially holds" refers to the individual that was formally appointed, confirmed (if confirmation is required), and sworn in as the head of state of Venezuela or otherwise confirmed by official government information as being the head of state of Venezuela. 

If the Venezuelan government does not clearly state who is the head of state, the market will resolve to the individual who is listed as the Head of State by the UN (see: https://www.un.org/dgacm/en/content/protocol/hshgnfa).  

In the event that more than one official head of state is listed by the Venezuelan  government, this market will resolve to the individual who is listed as having primary status. If no distinction is made, the market will resolve to the individual who first assumed the position. 

If no individual holds the position this market will resolve to “No Head of State”. 

The following do NOT constitute "officially holding" the role: nominated, announced, or designated as the head of state of Venezuela but appointment not yet effective; appointed with an effective date after Dec 31, 2026; previously served as the head of state of Venezuela but term has expired, resigned, or been terminated before Dec 31, 2026; serving as the head of state of Venezuela in a consultant, contractor, or unofficial capacity; named as successor or heir apparent to the head of state of Venezuela but not yet appointed; or holding a different role with oversight of the head of state of Venezuela but not the specified position itself.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the UN recognized government of Venezuela. If the Government of Venezuela does not clearly state who is the head of state, information from the UN and a consensus of credible reporting may be used. 

Note: this market is mutually exclusive.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket favors Nicolás Maduro at 63% to remain Venezuela's head of state by end-2026, driven by a recent U.S. court ruling on April 28 allowing him to fund his defense amid ongoing charges following his January 3 capture by U.S. forces, coupled with persistent UN recognition of his legitimacy despite Delcy Rodríguez's interim presidency. Rodríguez holds 24% as vice president-turned-acting leader, sworn in January 5 with military backing and U.S. sanction relief, evidenced by resumed direct commercial flights and energy firm returns on May 1-3 signaling short-term stability but no election timeline. Opposition leader María Corina Machado trails at 8%, reflecting stalled transition and institutional continuity under PSUV control, with no snap election or coalition shifts announced.

markets.question: Will Khamenei post 40-44 posts from May 1 to May 8, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Khamenei (@khamenei_ir), posts on X between May 1, 12:00 PM ET and May 8, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies which are recorded on the main feed will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

The resolution source for this market is the "Post Counter" figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a low volume of posts from the official @khamenei_ir X account—archival statements from the late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei—at under 5 (56.5%) or 5-9 (41.5%) during May 1-8, driven by its prolonged silence since the April 13 post amid the leadership transition following his February 28 martyrdom in US-Israeli strikes. Recent US intelligence reports detailing severe injuries to successor Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei, including limited official access, have fueled expectations of subdued activity, contrasting the new @MKhamenei_ir account's May 1 cluster of nine posts marking Workers' and Teachers' Days. Earlier April markets resolved around 10-14 posts, but three weeks without updates signal trader caution on resumption.

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the NV-01 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NV-01 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

Dina Titus's commanding fundraising lead—$1.5 million raised and over $1 million cash-on-hand—over Democratic primary challengers Joy Hoover, Gabriel Cornejo, and Luis Paniagua bolsters trader consensus for a Democratic hold at 84% in Nevada's 1st Congressional District, a D+2 partisan lean area. markets.question: Will the price of Solana be above $100 on May 10?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for SOL/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title has a final "Close" price higher than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the SOL/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/SOL_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance SOL/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

Price precision is determined by the number of decimal places in the source.

markets.question: Will Moldova be in the top 3 at Eurovision 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the named country finishes Eurovision 2026 as one of the top 3 highest scoring candidates.

If at any point it is impossible for the listed candidate to finish as one of the top 3 highest scorers based on the rules of the competition (i.e. they are eliminated), this market may immediately resolve to "No".

If no winner is announced by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

Withdrawals by Iceland, Ireland, Netherlands, Slovenia, and Spain over Israel participation slim the 35-entry field, amplifying diaspora televote swings. markets.question: Will Washington use a new congressional map for the 2026 United States midterm elections?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed US state uses a new congressional district map for the 2026 United States midterm elections. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

To qualify, after the 2024 United States congressional elections, the listed state must have adopted a new congressional district map that is:
- Formally adopted and enacted into law by the appropriate legislative or redistricting authority;
- Not enjoined, vacated or otherwise fully struck down prior to the 2026 United States Midterm elections; and
- In effect for use in the 2026 United States midterm elections.

Maps that are temporarily stayed pending appeal but later upheld will qualify if they are in effect for use in the 2026 United States midterm elections.

Maps that are completely redrawn by a court, special master, or legislature before implementation will not qualify, unless the redrawn map is itself a new map used in the 2026 United States midterm elections. 

If the 2026 United States midterm elections occur and no new qualifying map is in use, this market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution sources for this market will be official information from the listed state and a consensus of credible reporting.
